claim: US nominal GDP ~$30.5-30.8T vs China ~$19.2-19.6T (2025); US ~1.5-1.6x larger nominal; China larger on PPP (Statista; StatisticsTimes; PIIE)
China fell from ~77% of US nominal GDP (2021) to ~65% (2026); growth ~4.4-5% vs US ~1.6-2.5%; China population peaked 2022 (Euronews; NextBigFuture; StatsPanda)

Acemoglu task-framework estimate: AI ~0.5-0.7% TFP over a decade vs McKinsey/Goldman 1.5-3.4%/yr; 2025 experimental work finds per-task gains ~3x Acemoglu's 27% parameter (NBER w32487; arXiv 2512.21316)
OpenResearch UBI ($1k/mo for 3yr): recipients stayed employed, worked slightly fewer hours, spent more on needs; well-being not clearly improved (Bloomberg; Fortune 2024)
Alaska Permanent Fund (1976) pays an annual dividend; Bruenig "American Solidarity Fund" social-wealth-fund proposal (People's Policy Project; Jacobin; The Intercept)
World/Worldcoin launched 2023 with crypto-UBI framing; pivoted to enterprise proof-of-humanity by 2026, dropping the UBI goal (Biometric Update; AMBCrypto)
Renewal prescription: social wealth fund + universal basic dividend + land value tax as the "plug" for wealth's escape into equities/land; each component carries its own counter-view
Recursive robotics/automation loop compounds toward falling unit labor costs
verifier: signpost: by 2031 — mfg unit-labor-cost + TFP threshold [numbers TBD]

claim: Governance thesis: AI-driven abundance is net democracy-preserving
Governance counter-claim (entered at equal prominence): AI-driven abundance enables techno-authoritarian concentration of power

Automated reshoring is the most durable path to sovereign, cost-competitive industrial capacity for a high-wage democracy: US wages won't fall enough to make manual domestic manufacturing competitive and offshoring cedes the base to a strategic rival, so automated domestic capacity is the only mix at once domestic, cheap, and adversary-independent — counter-view: subsidy/tariff reshoring (CHIPS Act/IRA) builds only behind a permanent cost wall, allied friend-shoring depends on partners' goodwill, high-value-design comparative advantage cedes the physical base; catch: sovereign cheap capacity is necessary but not sufficient (narrow ownership + no worker income → collapsed domestic demand → instability)

Wright's Law (experience curve): unit cost falls a roughly constant % per doubling of cumulative production; validated across 62 technologies (Nagy/Farmer/Bui/Trancik 2013) and ~51 (Lafond et al. 2018); WWII demand shock is a natural experiment establishing causal direction experience->cost; solar ~20% per doubling (PLoS ONE 2013; Technological Forecasting & Social Change 2018; Our World in Data)
